metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text:
After three financially strong years, sentiment in arable farming is turning around. The potato price is falling towards zero and prices for sugar beet and wheat are also disappointing. Over the months January to November, the liquidity position of the average arable farmer in the Netherlands decreased by 34,000 euros, according to transaction data from ABN AMRO. Download the complete report. Read all the facts and developments in the report 'Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices'.
[Arable farming liquidity monitor (December 23, 2025). 202 KB](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/liquiditeitsmonitor-akkerbouw-december-2025.pdf).

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text:
The potato price is falling towards zero and prices for sugar beet and wheat are also disappointing. Over the months January to November, the liquidity position of the average arable farmer in the Netherlands decreased by 34,000 euros, according to transaction data from ABN AMRO. Download the complete report. Read all the facts and developments in the report 'Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices'.
[Arable farming liquidity monitor (December 23, 2025). 202 KB](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/liquiditeitsmonitor-akkerbouw-december-2025.pdf). Liquidity, the amount in the current account, is an important indicator to determine whether an arable farmer can meet his obligations in the short term.

text: Wageningen University & Research estimates the average income of arable farmers in 2025 at 60,000 euros. Potato market down hard. Due to high potato prices in 2024, arable farmers had reserved a relatively large share of the 2025 cultivation plan for potatoes. metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Potato market down hard. Due to high potato prices in 2024, arable farmers had reserved a relatively large share of the 2025 cultivation plan for potatoes. Partly as a result, the potato area of ​​the four major European potato countries increased by 7 percent. Due to disappointing demand from fast food chains and declining exports abroad, stocks in cold stores were not depleted in the first quarter of 2025. Overcapacity was lurking.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to high potato prices in 2024, arable farmers had reserved a relatively large share of the 2025 cultivation plan for potatoes. Partly as a result, the potato area of ​​the four major European potato countries increased by 7 percent. Due to disappointing demand from fast food chains and declining exports abroad, stocks in cold stores were not depleted in the first quarter of 2025. Overcapacity was lurking. Due to favorable weather conditions, the growing season started early and the yield per hectare was exceptionally high.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Partly as a result, the potato area of ​​the four major European potato countries increased by 7 percent. Due to disappointing demand from fast food chains and declining exports abroad, stocks in cold stores were not depleted in the first quarter of 2025. Overcapacity was lurking. Due to favorable weather conditions, the growing season started early and the yield per hectare was exceptionally high. This reinforced the oversupply, causing the potato market to collapse further.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to disappointing demand from fast food chains and declining exports abroad, stocks in cold stores were not depleted in the first quarter of 2025. Overcapacity was lurking. Due to favorable weather conditions, the growing season started early and the yield per hectare was exceptionally high. This reinforced the oversupply, causing the potato market to collapse further. As a result of overcapacity, the potato price on the open market moved to only a few cents per kg, see Figure 2.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: As a result of overcapacity, the potato price on the open market moved to only a few cents per kg, see Figure 2. Potatoes were even sold as animal feed. Roughly 80 percent of potato sales are on average via contract prices. The grower is therefore provided with guaranteed sales and fixed prices. But the remaining 20 percent – ​​where sales are via the free market – saw price formation collapse.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

Most arable farmers have a mix of fixed contract prices and free market prices, so the impact is not too bad.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: The grower is therefore provided with guaranteed sales and fixed prices. But the remaining 20 percent – ​​where sales are via the free market – saw price formation collapse. Most arable farmers have a mix of fixed contract prices and free market prices, so the impact is not too bad. Potatoes account for 35 percent of an arable farm's income. The loss in potato cultivation can only be compensated to a limited extent by other crops: sugar beets, however, fell sharply due to strong production within Europe in combination with increased sugar imports as a result of reduced duties on products from outside the European Union (EU).

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Potatoes account for 35 percent of an arable farm's income. The loss in potato cultivation can only be compensated to a limited extent by other crops: sugar beets, however, fell sharply due to strong production within Europe in combination with increased sugar imports as a result of reduced duties on products from outside the European Union (EU). Grain prices are also low due to high global inventories. Costs are rising faster than income. Income increased by 95,000 euros over the period January to November compared to the same period last year.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

Grain prices are also low due to high global inventories. Costs are rising faster than income. metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Costs are rising faster than income. Income increased by 95,000 euros over the period January to November compared to the same period last year. Revenues were highest in the second quarter due to final stock sales from the 2024 harvest. In reality, these are transactions from the first quarter, but revenues are only collected later. metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Income increased by 95,000 euros over the period January to November compared to the same period last year. Revenues were highest in the second quarter due to final stock sales from the 2024 harvest. In reality, these are transactions from the first quarter, but revenues are only collected later. In addition, many arable farmers received compensation from livestock farmers who spread their manure over the fields in the spring. A third factor concerns the subsidies from the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP).

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Income from arable farming can therefore shift significantly over the months. Despite higher income, the liquidity position deteriorated as costs rose faster than income. Expenses increased by 136,000 euros compared to the same period a year earlier. High inflation caused costs to rise across the board. Rent, usually paid in April and October, rose sharply: the annual change rate was 4.58 percent.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Despite higher income, the liquidity position deteriorated as costs rose faster than income. Expenses increased by 136,000 euros compared to the same period a year earlier. High inflation caused costs to rise across the board. Rent, usually paid in April and October, rose sharply: the annual change rate was 4.58 percent. The second quarter has so-called spring costs such as the purchase of seeds and seed potatoes as well as costs for the contractor and land preparation.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Expenses increased by 136,000 euros compared to the same period a year earlier. High inflation caused costs to rise across the board. Rent, usually paid in April and October, rose sharply: the annual change rate was 4.58 percent. The second quarter has so-called spring costs such as the purchase of seeds and seed potatoes as well as costs for the contractor and land preparation. Due to a dry summer, growers were forced to fully use irrigation systems, which resulted in high fuel costs in June and July.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: High inflation caused costs to rise across the board. Rent, usually paid in April and October, rose sharply: the annual change rate was 4.58 percent. The second quarter has so-called spring costs such as the purchase of seeds and seed potatoes as well as costs for the contractor and land preparation. Due to a dry summer, growers were forced to fully use irrigation systems, which resulted in high fuel costs in June and July. Due to disappointing results in 2025, investments in, for example, new machines or expansion of storage capacity will be postponed.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Rent, usually paid in April and October, rose sharply: the annual change rate was 4.58 percent. The second quarter has so-called spring costs such as the purchase of seeds and seed potatoes as well as costs for the contractor and land preparation. Due to a dry summer, growers were forced to fully use irrigation systems, which resulted in high fuel costs in June and July. Due to disappointing results in 2025, investments in, for example, new machines or expansion of storage capacity will be postponed. This dampens the depressing effects on liquidity.

Due to disappointing results in 2025, investments in, for example, new machines or expansion of storage capacity will be postponed. This dampens the depressing effects on liquidity. Dutch acreage continues to grow.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to a dry summer, growers were forced to fully use irrigation systems, which resulted in high fuel costs in June and July. Due to disappointing results in 2025, investments in, for example, new machines or expansion of storage capacity will be postponed. This dampens the depressing effects on liquidity. Dutch acreage continues to grow. Due to overcrowded warehouses and cold stores, contract prices for potatoes are expected to be 10 to 20 percent lower in 2026 compared to 2025.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to disappointing results in 2025, investments in, for example, new machines or expansion of storage capacity will be postponed. This dampens the depressing effects on liquidity. Dutch acreage continues to grow. Due to overcrowded warehouses and cold stores, contract prices for potatoes are expected to be 10 to 20 percent lower in 2026 compared to 2025. Market parties hope to limit the potato area so that the market can regain balance.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: This dampens the depressing effects on liquidity. Dutch acreage continues to grow. Due to overcrowded warehouses and cold stores, contract prices for potatoes are expected to be 10 to 20 percent lower in 2026 compared to 2025. Market parties hope to limit the potato area so that the market can regain balance. Cosun, the sugar beet cooperative of Dutch growers, aims to do the same by giving members 10 percent fewer delivery notes for sugar beets.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Dutch acreage continues to grow. Due to overcrowded warehouses and cold stores, contract prices for potatoes are expected to be 10 to 20 percent lower in 2026 compared to 2025. Market parties hope to limit the potato area so that the market can regain balance. Cosun, the sugar beet cooperative of Dutch growers, aims to do the same by giving members 10 percent fewer delivery notes for sugar beets. This is at odds with the fact that the Dutch arable area is increasing as a result of the purchase scheme in the animal sectors.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to overcrowded warehouses and cold stores, contract prices for potatoes are expected to be 10 to 20 percent lower in 2026 compared to 2025. Market parties hope to limit the potato area so that the market can regain balance. Cosun, the sugar beet cooperative of Dutch growers, aims to do the same by giving members 10 percent fewer delivery notes for sugar beets. This is at odds with the fact that the Dutch arable area is increasing as a result of the purchase scheme in the animal sectors. Dairy farmers' land is released and can be used for extensification of remaining dairy farmers or sold or rented to arable farmers.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Market parties hope to limit the potato area so that the market can regain balance. Cosun, the sugar beet cooperative of Dutch growers, aims to do the same by giving members 10 percent fewer delivery notes for sugar beets. This is at odds with the fact that the Dutch arable area is increasing as a result of the purchase scheme in the animal sectors. Dairy farmers' land is released and can be used for extensification of remaining dairy farmers or sold or rented to arable farmers. Because the cultivation of potatoes and sugar beets is discouraged, we expect a shift within the cultivation plan to onions, carrots or cabbage crops.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Cosun, the sugar beet cooperative of Dutch growers, aims to do the same by giving members 10 percent fewer delivery notes for sugar beets. This is at odds with the fact that the Dutch arable area is increasing as a result of the purchase scheme in the animal sectors. Dairy farmers' land is released and can be used for extensification of remaining dairy farmers or sold or rented to arable farmers. Because the cultivation of potatoes and sugar beets is discouraged, we expect a shift within the cultivation plan to onions, carrots or cabbage crops. Major shifts in the cultivation plan can throw the market out of balance elsewhere, causing price pressure.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: This is at odds with the fact that the Dutch arable area is increasing as a result of the purchase scheme in the animal sectors. Dairy farmers' land is released and can be used for extensification of remaining dairy farmers or sold or rented to arable farmers. Because the cultivation of potatoes and sugar beets is discouraged, we expect a shift within the cultivation plan to onions, carrots or cabbage crops. Major shifts in the cultivation plan can throw the market out of balance elsewhere, causing price pressure. It remains a challenge for the arable farming sector to find balance in 2026.

Difference between income groups.

Major shifts in the cultivation plan can throw the market out of balance elsewhere, causing price pressure. It remains a challenge for the arable farming sector to find balance in 2026. metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Major shifts in the cultivation plan can throw the market out of balance elsewhere, causing price pressure. It remains a challenge for the arable farming sector to find balance in 2026. Difference between income groups. When we study income and expenditure for different income groups, it appears that the trend is similar across different income groups. metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: It remains a challenge for the arable farming sector to find balance in 2026. Difference between income groups. When we study income and expenditure for different income groups, it appears that the trend is similar across different income groups. In other words, we do not see a clear relationship in farm size and liquidity development, which makes the trends described here representative for arable farmers. An exception to this are companies in the highest income decile, which have a much more volatile liquidity trend.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Difference between income groups. When we study income and expenditure for different income groups, it appears that the trend is similar across different income groups. In other words, we do not see a clear relationship in farm size and liquidity development, which makes the trends described here representative for arable farmers. An exception to this are companies in the highest income decile, which have a much more volatile liquidity trend. This can be explained by the fact that they are mainly active on the free market and are therefore more sensitive to price fluctuations.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

In other words, we do not see a clear relationship in farm size and liquidity development, which makes the trends described here representative for arable farmers. An exception to this are companies in the highest income decile, which have a much more volatile liquidity trend. This can be explained by the fact that they are mainly active on the free market and are therefore more sensitive to price fluctuations. The data from this analysis comes from 271 specialized arable farms in the Netherlands.

metadata.source_title: Arable farming relies on reserves due to the fall in potato market prices

text: Due to the poor yields in 2025, the arable farmer is consuming his reserves, which means we see a slight increase in companies that are temporarily in the red, see Figure 5. Nevertheless, the share of companies that are in the red remains historically low. In the second quarter of 2026, we expect a limited peak in liquidity shortages due to high spring costs such as land preparation and sowing crops in combination with limited income. Depending on yields per hectare and crop prices, this peak will gradually decrease in the summer of 2026. An increasing share of companies that are in the red is not immediately alarming, because arable farmers are historically used to volatility in yields over the years.

An increasing share of companies that are in the red is not immediately alarming, because arable farmers are historically used to volatility in yields over the years. Most companies currently have substantial reserves on which they can draw, unless they have recently made large investments from their own resources.
